behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff: diff extraction -> review-input builder. MOCK-MISS: this is not just one pure function if the scope is assembled across components. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; fixture diffs/files are enough. LLM-DEP: correctness here is about what text is sent, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is only about input scoping, not real API/model outcomes.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PR-processing modules together with a fixture PR diff and file contents, intercept the payload sent to the reviewer stage, and assert only changed hunks are included while unchanged file regions are excluded.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: correct diff-position attachment depends on real GitHub PR diff semantics/API behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked comment APIs can accept invalid positions that real GitHub rejects or remaps. Not Agentic because this is position-mapping correctness, not model output quality. Not Workflow because no multi-agentic sequence is required.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR on GitHub, post inline comments using computed positions, then read back PR review comments and verify each appears on the intended file/hunk/line in the actual diff view metadata.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: pass/fail depends on real model judgment quality (finding a real bug), which is non-deterministic and cannot be validated with deterministic stubs. Rejected Workflow because behavior asks for a single outcome (detect at least one defect), not a required multi-agentic chain. Rejected System as minimum because the core failure boundary is model output quality, not transport/infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a benchmark PR diff with a seeded known bug, call the real model-powered reviewer, and assert at least one returned finding correctly targets that bug class/location (allowing repeated runs due to stochasticity).

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module consistency between inline-comment posting results and summary composer state. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only formatter logic can miss mismatch at component boundaries. REAL-INFRA: no real network is required; in-process fake GitHub client can simulate post results/failures. LLM-DEP: correspondence check is deterministic bookkeeping, not model quality. MULTI-STEP agentic chain is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute orchestrator with deterministic findings and a fake GitHub client that records inline posts; generate summary and assert every summary item has a matching posted inline comment id/location and no orphan summary entries exist.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires the full end-to-end agent pipeline on a real PR. It combines infrastructure-dependent steps (GitHub API) and agentic review generation in sequence. By definition this is a full agent pipeline, so Workflow is the minimum boundary. Rejected lower levels because they each isolate only part of the sequence and can miss cross-step orchestration failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the automation unattended against a real test PR: fetch diff, invoke real reviewer, post inline comments, then post summary; assert successful completion plus expected artifacts/order in GitHub (inline comments exist, summary exists, run exits cleanly).
